data.body:
Recognition didn’t match her impact. The Lederbergs shared the 1956 Pasteur Award. Yet, in 1958 he shared the Nobel Prize with Beadle & Tatum, but didn't even mention her in his acceptance speech. 

A stark example of the Matilda Effect — the overlooking of women’s scientific contributions.
9/10
